transaction: A walk-in customer with no account requests a cash transaction of ₹75,000 at a bank branch.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - occasional transaction above ₹50,000 threshold requires full KYC
expected_actions: Carry out full KYC / customer identification before processing
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q2(b): walk-in occasional transaction >= ₹50,000 triggers KYC

transaction: An international money transfer of $20,000 is requested by a walk-in customer to a beneficiary overseas.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - international money transfer requires KYC regardless of amount
expected_actions: Verify source of funds and beneficiary
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q2(c): any international money transfer triggers KYC

transaction: A customer wants to purchase a Demand Draft for ₹60,000 by paying cash.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - DD/Pay Order of ₹50,000 and above cannot be issued against cash
expected_actions: Require debit to customer account or payment by cheque
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q30: DD/PO >= ₹50,000 must be by account debit or cheque, not cash

transaction: A customer wants to purchase a Demand Draft for ₹20,000 by paying cash.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - DD/Pay Order below ₹50,000 may be purchased against cash
expected_actions: Process the transaction; amount is below the cash threshold
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q30: DD/PO below ₹50,000 can be purchased against cash

transaction: An individual wants to open a savings account but does not possess any Officially Valid Document (OVD).
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - Small Account provisions for customers without an OVD
expected_actions: Open as a Small Account with self-attested photograph and officer certification
Apply the 12-month operational limit and conditions
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q6: Small Account when customer has no OVD

transaction: An individual opens a current account and provides a passport and PAN card.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - individual account opening with valid OVD and PAN
expected_actions: Proceed with account opening; required OVD and PAN provided
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q5: passport is a valid OVD; PAN required

transaction: A customer presents a banker's cheque dated five months ago for payment.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - payment instruments presented beyond three months are not honoured
expected_actions: Decline payment; instrument is beyond the three-month validity period
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q31: cheque/draft/pay order invalid beyond 3 months from issuance

transaction: A bank suspects that the identity documents submitted by an existing customer may not be authentic.
expected_regulations: RBI KYC - KYC must be re-performed when authenticity or adequacy of customer data is in doubt
source_rule: KYC09062025 Q2(d): doubt about authenticity/adequacy triggers KYC

expected_actions: Request missing details: amount, counterparty, jurisdiction, instrument type
source_rule: Insufficient information - sufficiency gate should fire

transaction: A retail client in Germany is sold a complex structured derivative without an appropriateness assessment under MiFID II.
expected_actions: Flag for human review - MiFID II rules are not in the ingested corpus
source_rule: Out-of-corpus: no MiFID II document ingested; should not fabricate an assessment
